purpose: Deliver a real Visual Studio Code extension for Ananta as a first-class developer surface: goal submission, analysis/review commands, task/artifact visibility, approvals, audit/repair visibility, TUI launch and safe backend-driven workflows.
depends_on: Neovim runtime MVP as editor-integration reference
goal: VS Code should become the main mainstream editor integration for Ananta, supporting common developer workflows without duplicating hub orchestration, governance, approval, audit or repair logic.
success_criteria: VS Code extension project exists with reproducible build/package/test setup.
Extension can configure Ananta endpoint/profile/auth safely.
Extension can check backend health and capabilities.
Command Palette exposes Ananta goal/analyze/review/patch/new/evolve workflows.
Editor context capture is bounded, visible and user-confirmed before send.
Sidebar or activity view shows tasks, artifacts, approvals and runtime status.
TUI can be launched safely from VS Code terminal.
No file mutation, repair execution or approval action happens implicitly.
Tests and smoke checks prove core runtime behavior.
architecture_guardrails: VS Code extension is a thin client, not a second Ananta control plane.
All orchestration, planning, policy, approval, audit and repair enforcement stay in the Ananta backend.
All risky actions require explicit user confirmation and backend permission.
Editor context must be bounded and inspectable.
Secrets/tokens must not be logged, shown in errors or passed unsafely via command line.
Browser fallback remains available for deep/high-risk admin workflows.
TUI launch must be explicit and observable.

milestones.title: Editor context and command palette workflows
Sidebar views for tasks, artifacts and approvals
Runtime, audit, repair and TUI integration
Docs, smoke checks and release readiness

tasks.title: VS Code plugin scope boundary define
acceptance_criteria: Document defines MVP, advanced and browser-fallback features.
VS Code is explicitly defined as thin client surface.
Dangerous workflows such as repair execution, config writes and file mutation are backend-gated or deferred.
Scope separates editor workflows from operator/admin workflows.

tasks.title: VS Code extension project skeleton create
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains real VS Code extension project directory, e.g. client_surfaces/vscode_extension.
package.json exists with activationEvents, contributes.commands and contributes.views where needed.
TypeScript project config exists.
Extension can compile from clean checkout.

tasks.title: Build and packaging setup implement
acceptance_criteria: npm scripts exist for compile, test, lint and package.
VSIX packaging path is documented.
Build is deterministic and CI-friendly.
Build failure blocks runtime_mvp claim.

tasks.title: Extension architecture document create
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain extension modules: backend client, context capture, commands, views, webviews, terminal/TUI integration.
Docs show which existing Ananta APIs are reused.
Docs explicitly forbid client-side policy/orchestration duplication.
Docs list future advanced features separately.

tasks.title: Frontend API reuse mapping create
acceptance_criteria: Angular Hub API surface is mapped to VS Code extension features.
Each planned view/action lists the backend endpoint/read model it uses.
Missing endpoints are listed explicitly.
No fake local-only data model is used for runtime claims.

tasks.title: VS Code configuration settings implement
acceptance_criteria: Extension settings include Ananta base URL and profile/runtime target.
Auth mode is defined safely.
Raw tokens are not stored in normal plain settings if secret storage is available.
Invalid config is visible and actionable.

tasks.title: Secret storage and auth handling implement
acceptance_criteria: VS Code SecretStorage or equivalent safe mechanism is used for tokens/secrets.
Logs and errors redact secrets.
Missing/invalid auth returns explicit degraded state.
Tests cover missing token, invalid token and redaction.

tasks.title: Backend client implement
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript backend client supports health, capabilities, goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals, audit, repair and config read endpoints where available.
Client has timeout handling.
Client maps backend failures to typed degraded states.
Client does not silently swallow backend errors.

tasks.title: Capability and permission handshake implement
acceptance_criteria: Extension checks backend health/capabilities before enabling commands.
Denied or unsupported actions are disabled or clearly marked.
Command execution rechecks capability where needed.
No action appears available when backend denies it.

tasks.title: Runtime status bar and diagnostics implement
acceptance_criteria: Status bar shows connected/disconnected/degraded state.
Click opens Ananta status/details view.
Diagnostics show backend unreachable, auth failed, capability missing and policy denied states.
Status never shows success if backend is degraded.

tasks.title: Bounded editor context capture implement
acceptance_criteria: Extension captures active file path, selected text, language id and workspace root.
Context size is bounded.
No unrelated files are included implicitly.
Likely secrets are blocked, redacted or warned before send.

tasks.title: Context preview and confirmation UI implement
acceptance_criteria: User can preview what context will be sent.
Preview shows file path, selection length, excerpt and metadata.
Sensitive or large context warnings are visible.
Goal submission can be cancelled before sending.

tasks.title: Command Palette goal workflows implement
acceptance_criteria: Commands exist for Ananta: Submit Goal, Analyze Selection, Review File, Patch Plan, New Project and Evolve Project.
Commands map to official backend goal/task flows.
Command payload includes explicit mode/profile/context metadata.
Success result links to task/artifact views or web UI.

tasks.title: Quick goal input panel implement
acceptance_criteria: Quick input supports goal text and optional mode selection.
Input validates empty/too-large values.
Selected context is summarized before submit.
Submission shows status and created task IDs.

tasks.title: Editor context menu actions implement
acceptance_criteria: Context menu actions exist for selected text/current file where appropriate.
Actions remain capability-aware.
No action mutates files directly.
Context menu actions reuse the same safe context preview flow.

tasks.title: Result rendering and notification flow implement
acceptance_criteria: Command results show clear success/failure/degraded notification.
Results can open task/artifact detail in VS Code side view.
Browser fallback is available.
Unsupported result types degrade to text/browser fallback.

tasks.title: Ananta activity bar and sidebar container implement
acceptance_criteria: VS Code activity bar container exists for Ananta.
Sidebar has sections for Status, Goals/Tasks, Artifacts, Approvals and Runtime.
Views refresh manually.
Views handle empty/degraded states.

tasks.title: Tasks and goals tree view implement
acceptance_criteria: Tree view lists recent goals and tasks.
Items show status, mode, team/profile where available.
Selecting item opens detail panel or webview.
Refresh and filter by status are supported.

tasks.title: Task detail and log view implement
acceptance_criteria: Task detail shows title, status, summary, trace, related artifacts and next step.
Task logs can be loaded or refreshed where backend supports them.
Stale/missing task state is explicit.
No task execution happens implicitly.

tasks.title: Artifact view implement
acceptance_criteria: Artifact list/detail is available in sidebar or panel.
Text artifacts render inside VS Code.
Diff/review artifacts render read-only.
Binary/rich artifacts use browser fallback.

tasks.title: Approval and review queue view implement
acceptance_criteria: Pending approval/review items are visible with enough context.
Approve/reject actions require explicit confirmation.
Actions call backend approval APIs only.
Stale/denied/already-handled states are handled safely.

tasks.title: System and runtime overview view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows backend health, capabilities, active profile/governance and provider/model summary where available.
View is read-only by default.
Sensitive values are redacted.
Unavailable endpoints degrade clearly.

tasks.title: Audit explorer MVP implement
acceptance_criteria: Extension can list audit log summaries where backend supports it.
Audit entries redact sensitive data.
Entries can link to related goal/task/artifact/trace where available.
Deep audit analysis uses browser fallback if needed.

tasks.title: Repair visibility MVP implement
acceptance_criteria: Repair sessions or repair-related task states are visible where backend exposes them.
Diagnosis, proposed steps, dry-run status, approval state and verification result are shown where available.
No repair execution is triggered by opening or refreshing the view.
Repair approval/execution actions are deferred unless backend-gated and explicitly confirmed.

tasks.title: Launch Ananta TUI in VS Code terminal implement
acceptance_criteria: Command launches documented Ananta TUI command in VS Code integrated terminal.
Launch is explicit and user-triggered.
Endpoint/profile context is passed safely.
Tokens/secrets are not exposed in command line where avoidable.

tasks.title: Open Ananta Web UI fallback implement
acceptance_criteria: Commands can open relevant Ananta Web UI pages.
Fallback exists for tasks, artifacts, audit, config and repair views.
Browser fallback is clearly labeled and never bypasses policy.
URLs are generated safely from configured base URL.

tasks.title: Security and governance negative tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify no client-side approval/policy bypass.
Tests verify denied, unauthorized, stale and malformed backend responses.
Tests verify no implicit file mutation.
Tests verify no implicit repair execution.

tasks.title: Secret redaction and context safety tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify tokens/secrets are redacted in logs/errors/output.
Tests cover likely secret patterns in selected text/context.
Tests verify size limits and provenance metadata.
Tests verify unrelated files are not included implicitly.

tasks.title: Backend client unit and contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover health, capabilities, goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals and degraded responses.
Backend response shape assumptions are locked by fixtures.
Timeout and malformed response behavior is tested.
Tests run without live backend.

tasks.title: VS Code command and view tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover command registration.
Tests cover command dispatch with mocked backend client.
Tests cover view model rendering inputs.
Tests cover capability-disabled commands.

tasks.title: Extension smoke test add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test verifies extension activation.
Smoke test verifies command registration.
Smoke test verifies mocked health check and one goal submission flow.
Smoke test is runnable locally and suitable for CI.

tasks.title: CI lane for VS Code extension add
acceptance_criteria: CI runs extension compile/tests.
CI reports extension failures separately from Python backend failures.
VS Code extension runtime claims are blocked if CI/smoke evidence is missing.
Packaging step is optional but documented.

tasks.title: VS Code user guide write
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain install/build/run for development.
Docs explain settings, auth and connection status.
Docs list available commands and views.
Docs explain safe context preview and browser/TUI fallback.

tasks.title: Developer smoke checklist write
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers install extension, configure backend, health check, analyze selection, review file, submit goal, view task/artifact and launch TUI.
Checklist includes expected success and failure signals.
Checklist distinguishes automated vs manual checks.
Checklist is short enough for release review.

tasks.title: Runtime inventory and audit integration update
acceptance_criteria: Client surface runtime inventory includes vscode_plugin status.
Audit script checks VS Code extension runtime evidence.
Runtime evidence includes package.json, source files, build command and smoke/test evidence.
Foundation-only VS Code files cannot satisfy runtime_mvp.

tasks.title: VS Code merge readiness report generate
acceptance_criteria: Report lists implemented commands, views and deferred features.
Report includes build/test/smoke evidence.
Report lists known backend/API gaps if any.
Report states merge-safe vs feature-flag-only recommendation.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define VS Code scope and bootstrap real extension project.
Stage 2: Implement secure connection/auth and backend client.
Stage 3: Implement bounded editor context and command palette workflows.
Stage 4: Implement sidebar views for goals/tasks/artifacts/approvals.
Stage 5: Add runtime/audit/repair visibility and TUI/Web UI fallback.
Stage 6: Add security, contract, command, view and smoke tests.
Stage 7: Update docs, inventory, audit and merge-readiness report.

VSC-M3.title: Editor context and command palette workflows

VSC-M4.title: Sidebar views for tasks, artifacts and approvals

VSC-M5.title: Runtime, audit, repair and TUI integration

VSC-M7.title: Docs, smoke checks and release readiness

notes: Third implementation bundle VSC-T16..VSC-T21 was completed and tracked to done state.
Result detail rendering, sidebar sections, tasks/artifacts detail views and approval queue actions are now reflected in task completion state.
tasks_status_summary and execution_stage_summary were synchronized after milestone transitions (VSC-M3 done, VSC-M4 done).

summary_notes: This track should not be mixed into the Eclipse advanced runtime track.
VS Code is likely the most important mainstream editor integration and should probably run before Eclipse Advanced.
MVP should focus on command palette, bounded context, task/artifact views, approval visibility and TUI launch.
Repair execution, config writes and deep admin workflows should remain browser-first or backend-gated until explicitly hardened.
Current todo file now contains synchronized status counters and explicit stage-level rollup data.
Milestones VSC-M1 through VSC-M4 are complete; remaining work now starts with runtime/audit/repair/TUI integration.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die aktuelle todo.json beschreibt den VS-Code-Track mit 36 Tasks in 7 Meilensteinen.
Inhaltlich war der Track konsistent strukturiert, aber einzelne Summary-Counter waren numerisch falsch.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: tasks_status_summary.by_priority wurde auf P0=25 und P1=11 korrigiert.
tasks_status_summary.by_risk wurde auf medium=10 und critical=11 korrigiert.
updated_at/checked_at wurden auf den aktuellen Bearbeitungszeitpunkt aktualisiert.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen und Erweiterungen
items: analysis_checks ergänzt, damit Korrekturen und Prüfstatus nachvollziehbar dokumentiert sind.
summary_notes erweitert, um den jetzt synchronisierten Status der Datei explizit festzuhalten.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Drittes Umsetzungsbündel ist abgeschlossen: insgesamt 21 done, 4 milestones done (VSC-M1 bis VSC-M4), 0 milestones in_progress.
Die Datei bleibt validator-konsistent und enthält aktualisierte Stage- sowie End-Summaries.
